title: Bind mount in a volume?
body:
### Issue Description

I would like to use a bind mount into a folder that I'm sharing with my container (https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/18220):
```
/home/user/project/data <= volume
/home/user/project/data/media <= external bind volume
```
It does work under Docker, but not under Podman when the container itself is run as rootless (so both Podman and the container: `-u 1000:1000 --uidmap 0:1:1 --uidmap 1000:0:1 --gidmap 0:1:1 --gidmap 1000:0:1`). The `/data/media` directory is just empty inside the container. Removing uidmap/gidmap, makes it visible again, however this does not follow my host user (1000), which I do want.

I could use the following workaround, as in simple mount each path individually, now it's visible even when including the uidmap/gidmap:
```
/home/user/project/data/media,U,Z <= volume (external bind volume)
/home/user/project/data/images,U,Z <= mounted volume
```
However I would need to do this for every directory.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. sudo mount --bind /path/to/media /home/user/project/data/media
2.  `-v /home/user/project/data:/data:U,z, -u 1000:1000 --uidmap 0:1:1 --uidmap 1000:0:1 --gidmap 0:1:1 --gidmap 1000:0:1`
3. `/data/media` in the container is empty


### Describe the results you received

Empty bind directory

### Describe the results you expected

Bind that works

comments.body:
What are the permission on the directory (including parent directories)?

This may be related: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/17823

cc @giuseppe

could you try restarting the podman pause process `podman system migrate`?  I am afraid the rootless user namespace is not getting the new mount propagated.  It might be caused by not using MS_SHARED for the parent mount

@Luap99 
```bash
$ cd /path/to/project && ll -Z
drwxr-xr-x. 1 user user system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0            64 17 apr 10:14 data
```

```bash
$ cd /path/to/project && ll -Z data
drwxr-sr-x. 1 user user system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0 550 17 apr 17:07 media
```

@giuseppe Could you please be more specific? :)
I'm new to Podman, should I try the MS_SHARED mount flag?

Thanks for looking into this! I've moved from Docker and so far, par this, everything works really smooth and rootless works great.
Just try running `podman system migrate` first and run again the command, let's see if that is the reason before digging further into this possible cause

could you show the output of `findmnt -R -o PROPAGATION,TARGET /` and `podman unshare findmnt -R -o PROPAGATION,TARGET /`?

I think you might have some mounts that are not using the `shared` propagation, so when a new mount happens on the host, it is not propagated inside the rootless mount namespace.  To see the new mount we need to destroy the old namespace and recreate it, so that it "clones" the current mounts on the host.
